worldBackground: The Star Wars Galaxy in 5BBY--A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away....The Galactic Empire was established 14 years ago in 19BBY. The Imperial Senate is but a vestige of the former Republic. There are whispers of rebellion, but only disparate, disorganized rebel cells--nothing coming close to approximating a Rebel Alliance. Imperial rule is stifling for some, crushing for others--but the propeganda has a point: some so-called 'freedom fighters' do look a lot like terrorists even to fair-minded Core-worlders.
questGenerationGuidance:
## 0. DO NOT CONTRADICT STAR WARS CANON OR TIMELINE

This is the Star Wars galaxy set in 5BBY

## 1. GROUND QUESTS IN THE NARRATIVE

Keep quest content grounded in the story. Never contradict or excessively embellish the narrative. Use existing factions, locations, characters, and worldlore. Never make up factions; either use existing factions directly or making a splinter group or offshoot.

## 2. STANDALONE OR ARC

If a quest does not tie into a larger pressure or tension in the world, such as a struggle against an opposing faction or group, make the quest standalone, without an arc, by setting arcId to null. Begin a new arc only when the story establishes a named antagonist, faction, or threat that clearly can't resolve in one quest. Impersonal sources like postings are usually standalone.

## 3. SCOPE AND RESTRAINT

Match complexity to what triggered the quest. A rat job is a rat job. A regional conflict gets regional weight. Don't seed hooks, conspiracies or mysteries the story hasn't earned. Some embellishment and invention is fine, but it must be supported by existing themes in the story.

## 4. LANGUAGE AND FRAMING

Star Wars (Science-Fantasy) World. 

## 5. QUEST OBJECTIVES AND STATEMENTS

Objectives: one imperative verb. Fight, rescue, retrieve, escort, infiltrate, negotiate, defend, investigate, sabotage, compete. No compound clauses with 'and' or 'or.'

Statements: WHO gave it, WHERE to go, WHAT to do, REWARD if stated. Two to three sentences.

## 6. THE BRIEF

Operational, not literary. State what exists in the world and what stands in the way.

Scale length to weight: a delivery gets one or two sentences; a regional arc gets a few dense ones. Do not pad with filler.

## 7. LOCATIONS AND AREAS

Regions are galactic sectors, Locations are planets, and areas are points of interest on a planet. Default to existing locations (planets) and areas (points of interest). Only generate new locations/areas when the quest demands it — and if you do, generate an in-universe Star Wars planet/area, state why in the brief.

## 8. CHARACTERS

Generate only what the quest requires — don't add NPCs to fill output. Never describe characters in a circumstantial way influenced by the events of the quest; instead, always make them evergreen, describing them as they would have been before the quest events.

## 9. ARC DESIGN

Only applies when an arc is legitimately created. Default shape: opposing group or antagonist with a base the player can reach. Physical destination, leadership to dismantle, clear resolution. Themes name the opposition: who they are, where they operate, what they want. Secrets funnel inward toward confrontation. Secrets must not contradict established NPC characterizations or Star Wars lore, invent factions, or escalate beyond the arc's actual scale. Ground secrets locally and proportionately. Other arc shapes exist — a personal vendetta, a spreading curse - but these still need a physical climax point and a concrete, concludable resolution condition. 'Find and destroy the cursed artifact causing the blight' is concludable. 'Understand the nature of the corruption' is not. Every arc must end somewhere a player can go and do something decisive.

## 10. AVOID

Do not generate: deep conspiracies behind simple quests, documents or ledgers as quest MacGuffins, NPCs added just to fill output, crystals or crystal-themed elements, shadow magic or shadow-themed creatures and elements, mysterious robed or hooded figures with undefined motives. MOST IMPORANTLY: DO NOT CONTRADICT STAR WARS CANON.
